prompt: Add code block syntax highlighting support

turns.content:
<task>
Add fenced code block parsing to the markdown converter. Blocks wrapped in ``` should be converted to <pre><code> HTML tags with optional language class.
</task>

1 import re 2 3 class MarkdownConverter: 4 def convert(self, text: str) -> str: 5 html = text 6 # Headers 7 html = re.sub(r'^### (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) 8 html = re.sub(r'^## (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) 9 html = re.sub(r'^# (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) 10 # Bold/italic 11 html = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', html) 12 html = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', html) 13 return html

arguments.summary: Read markdown2html/converter.py — MarkdownConverter.convert() handles headers (h1-h3) and bold/italic with regex substitutions. No code block support. Need to add fenced code block parsing (```lang ... ```) before other transformations.

next_steps.description: Add fenced code block regex to convert() — process before headers to avoid conflicts

arguments.content:
def convert(self, text: str) -> str: html = text # Fenced code blocks (must process first) html = re.sub( r'```(\w*)\n(.*?)```', self._code_block, html, flags=re.DOTALL, ) # Headers html = re.sub(r'^### (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) html = re.sub(r'^## (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) html = re.sub(r'^# (.+)$', r'

\1

', html, flags=re.M) # Bold/italic html = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', html) html = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', html) return html @staticmethod def _code_block(match): lang = match.group(1) code = match.group(2).rstrip('\n') lang_attr = f' class="language-{lang}"' if lang else '' return f'
{code}
'
